content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

July 11, 1995

Kenneth Oplinger
Business for Good Government
2201 Walnut Avenue, Suite 110
Fremont, CA 94538-2334

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-95-206

Dear Mr. Oplinger:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/ Since your letter refers to
activities which have already occurred, our advice must be general
in nature. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) It in no way is meant to
ratify any past conduct. We consider your letter to be a request
for informal assistance to clarify prior phone advice, pursuant to
Regulation 18329(c).2/

During our conversation on April 20, 1995, you indicated that
the Business for Good Government Committee had registered as a
state general purpose committee because it intends to be active at
the state level. However, you also stated that the Committee
intended to make some expenditures in connection with a June 6,
1995, election in the city of Fremont. We discussed the various
types of committees and I recommended that although you had
already registered as a state committee, you should file copies of
your campaign statements with the city during 1995 since the
Committee would be active in the local election.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Mr. Oplinger
Our File No. I-95-206
Page Two


     You represented in our conversation of June 28, 1995, that
the Committee has engaged in the following activity to date:

     April 20, 1995        $250 contribution to Jonelle Zager, a
                           candidate for Fremont City Council

     May 12, 1995          $400 independent expenditure for a
                           mailing containing get-out-the-vote
                           information and endorsing Jonelle Zager
                           ($300 for postage, $100 for printing)

     After the June 6     $45 payment for a newsletter to committee
     election              members containing information on the
                           election

     During 1995, state general purpose recipient committees are
not required to file regular pre-election statements.  City
general purpose recipient committees must file pre-election
statements during the six-month period in which the city election
is held only if they make contributions or independent expenditures
totaling $500 or more during the period covered by the pre-
election statement. (Section 84200.5.)

     The schedule for filing pre-election statements in connection
with the June 6, 1995 election was as follows:

     Type of Statement                Period Covered         Filing Deadline
                                      by Statement

     First Pre-election               Jan. 1 - April 22      April 27
     Second Pre-election              April 23 - May 20      May 25
     Third Pre-election3/             May 21 - May 30        June 2
     Semi-Annual                      May 31 - June 30       July 31

     If the Committee's activity is as you have represented, no
pre-election statements would have been triggered under the Act,
even if the Committee had filed the Form 410 as a city general
purpose recipient committee. State general purpose recipient
committees are not required to file statements with a city in
which the committee makes contributions. (Section 84215.)


_____________________________________________________________________
3/    Required pursuant to City of Fremont's local campaign
ordinance. Under Sections 84200.5 and 84200.8 of the Act, only
the first and second pre-election periods are considered for
purposes of determining whether pre-election statements are
required. The Commission has no authority to interpret or enforce
the city's ordinance.

Mr. Oplinger
Our File No. I-95-206
Page Three

However, since the Committee's activity for the first six
months of 1995 has been solely within the City of Fremont, we
recommend you file a copy of your semi-annual statement for the
period January 1, 1995, through June 30, 1995, with the Fremont
City Clerk's Office by July 31, 1995. A copy of Information
Manual C is enclosed for your review which outlines the filing
requirements for general purpose recipient committees.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign finance filing requirements for a state general purpose committee that is making contributions and independent expenditures in a local city election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Committee's activities did not trigger pre-election statement requirements under state law; however, the FPPC recommends filing a copy of the semi-annual statement with the Fremont City Clerk's Office by July 31, 1995, given the Committee's activity is solely within the City of Fremont.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter clarifying prior phone conversations dated April 20 and June 28, 1995. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; structure is narrative with discussion of facts and regulatory analysis. Document includes detailed filing schedules and footnotes explaining regulatory context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign finance filing requirements for a state general purpose committee that is making contributions and independent expenditures in a local city election?

CONCLUSION: The Committee's activities did not trigger pre-election statement requirements under state law; however, the FPPC recommends filing a copy of the semi-annual statement with the Fremont City Clerk's Office by July 31, 1995, given the Committee's activity is solely within the City of Fremont.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal advice to Business for Good Government Committee regarding campaign finance filing requirements for a state general purpose committee making expenditures in the June 6, 1995 Fremont city election. The letter clarifies prior phone advice and recommends filing the semi-annual statement with the city clerk's office.
